exemplar.title: The antimicrobial effects of 1M metal salts, Cu(NO3)2 Fe(NO3)3   Zn(NO3)2   Ni(NO3)2 and Pb(NO3)2  on the growth of bacteria.

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question is clearly stated, narrowly focused, and sustained throughout the report
Introduction convincingly locates the investigation in global context with coherent biochemical explanations
Methodology includes a well-structured variable table and appropriate statistical tests with rationale
weaknesses: Minor wording awkwardness in the research question and occasional grammatical lapses
Inconsistency between stated 1 M concentration and 0.05 M in Appendix C
Justification for concentration choice and trial number is brief, with minimal literature support
Conclusion does not explicitly revisit the research question or suggest future directions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs report demonstrates a clear, focused research question that drives the entire investigation and appropriate methodology with relevant controls and statistical tests. Although explanations are generally accurate and effective, minor wording issues, an inconsistency in reported concentrations, and thin methodological rationales prevent a perfect score. Overall performance aligns with the 5â6 band but merits a 5.

strengths: Effective application of primary literature to explain ROS generation and bacterial cell-wall differences
Clear use of statistical summaries (means and standard deviations) linked to data interpretation
Generally accurate use of key terminology such as âROS,â âgram-positive,â and âTukey HSDâ
weaknesses: Reliance on generic web sources rather than peer-reviewed literature
Omission of statistical test assumptions (e.g., homogeneity of variance) in ANOVA description
Occasional spelling and formatting errors (e.g., âEscheriaâ vs. âEscherichiaâ), inconsistent p-value notation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report shows mostly relevant and coherent application of sources and a generally accurate use of technical terminology, placing it firmly in the 3â4 band. However, uneven depth of source integration, use of non-scholarly citations, and minor inaccuracies justify a score of 4.

strengths: Data are processed and presented with appropriate statistical tests (ANOVA, post-hoc) and clear discussion of variability
Argument follows a logical progression from background to conclusion, revisiting the research question
Real-world applications are identified, demonstrating engagement with broader impact
weaknesses: Methodological inconsistencies (solution molarity discrepancy) undermine full rigor
Evaluation of anomalies and proposal of improvements remains cursory and descriptive
Ethical and environmental considerations are noted but lack depth and biosafety discussion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis is consistently relevant and coherent, with sound statistical interpretation and clear argument development. While some evaluation lacks depth and methodological inconsistencies persist, the work exceeds the middle of the 4â6 band, warranting a 6 for Critical Thinking.

strengths: Report structure is clear and complete, with logical headings and a comprehensive contents page
Formatting conventions (page numbers, word count, double spacing) are correctly applied
Tables and figures are generally placed in relevant sections, supporting readability
weaknesses: Several figures and graphs lack full captions, axis labels, and units
Table of contents entries are missing accurate page references
Alignment and cross-referencing issues interrupt the flow in places
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets core IB requirements and employs an appropriate structure, placing it in the 3â4 band. However, incomplete graph captions, missing page references in the table of contents, and minor formatting lapses prevent a top-band score, resulting in a 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay follows the IB EE structureâtitle page, contents, introduction, method, results, discussion, conclusion, references, appendicesâfacilitating navigation. Headings and sub-headings are sensibly organised, and tables/figures appear in relevant sections. Minor placement issues (in-text figure captions, page breaks) occasionally interrupt flow, but overall the structure is appropriate and complete, meeting the top band for Presentation-Structure.

The layout meets core EE requirements: 12-pt font, double spacing, page numbers, stated word count, and consistent Harvard referencing. Figures and tables are formatted but some lack full captions/units, and a few alignment issues arise. Retrieval dates are still missing for several web sources. These lapses indicate generally correct but not flawless application of conventions, meriting a lower score within the 3-4 band.

The report maintains an overall logical progression from background to conclusion, and the argument is generally coherent. Data are referenced to justify claims, and the research question is revisited. Nonetheless, sections contain redundancy, some methodological inconsistencies weaken logical force, and parts of the discussion repeat descriptive content rather than advancing analysis. These issues create minor breaks in coherence, situating the work in the mid 4-6 band with a score of 4.

The introduction locates the investigation convincingly within the global context of antimicrobial resistance and explains key biochemical ideas (ROS formation, bacterial cell-wall differences) with suitable diagrams and citations. However, repetitive phrasing, occasional grammatical lapses and some imprecise claims (e.g. âmakes them significant and valuableâ without evidence) limit complete clarity. Overall, the topic is communicated competently and with sufficient depth, meetingâbut not fully surpassingâthe descriptors for the 3-4 band.

A broad range of primary literature is cited to explain ROS generation, bacterial cell-wall structures and metal-ion mechanisms, and sources are linked to data interpretation in both analysis and conclusion. However, several citations are generic web pages; degrees of freedom and full statistical outputs are omitted; and some literature claims (e.g., everyday metal contexts) remain descriptive rather than critically integrated. The application is clear and mostly relevant but uneven in depth, aligning with the 3-4 band.

The data are processed correctly with means, standard deviations, graphical representation, ANOVA and relevant post-hoc tests. The student comments on variability, statistical significance and links findings to biochemical explanations (ROS, siderophores). Nevertheless, sample sizes are modest, some statistical values are dubious (e.g., p = .00000), and confusion over solution molarity undermines rigour. Analysis is therefore good but not consistently sophisticated, placing it mid-range of the 4-6 band.

The student describes a workable agar-well diffusion protocol, identifies variables in well-structured tables and justifies most controls. Statistical tests (ANOVA, Tukey, t-tests) are selected with brief rationale. Nevertheless, inconsistencies remain: the stated 1 M test concentration conflicts with the 0.05 M preparation in Appendix C; justification for five trials and well volume change is thin; and safety/ethical sections are minimal. These shortcomings indicate an adequate but not consistently thorough methodology, corresponding to the 3-4 band.

The evaluation identifies several pertinent limitations (ion vs. solid metals, concentration uncertainty, small sample size) and offers brief implications for validity. Conclusions are generally supported by data and statistics. However, explanations of anomalies are cursory, proposed improvements are limited, and ethical/environmental discussion is slight. The evaluation is competent but lacks the depth and critical insight characteristic of higher bands, fitting the 4â6 band at a lower score.

The research question is explicitly stated, narrowly delimited to five 1 M metal nitrates and two named bacterial species, and it guides the whole report. It is revisited in the analysis, comparison graph and conclusion, showing sustained focus. Minor wording awkwardness ("to what extentâ¦ able to inhibit") prevents a perfect score, but the clarity and sustained alignment with methods justify placement in the 5â6 band at a solid 5.

Key biological and statistical termsâROS, gram-positive, Tukey HSDâare generally used correctly and consistently. Occasional spelling/formatting errors (e.g., âEscheriaâ, inconsistent p-value notation) and some imprecise chemical concentration statements detract from complete accuracy. Overall usage is mostly accurate and appropriate, meriting the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The claim that metal salts âmake them significant and valuableâ is unsubstantiated; provide evidence or reference studies to support why these metals are particularly useful antimicrobial agents.
Figure 1 effectively illustrates the structural differences between Gram-positive and Gram-negative walls, supporting understanding of the experimental rationale.
The hypothesis section is truncated mid-sentence; ensure the full hypothesis is clearly stated, including expected differences between metal salts and bacterial species.
The methodology description blends preliminary and final protocols without clear separation or rationale; clarify each procedural step and explain why the agar-well diffusion method was chosen.
The research question is lengthy and phrased awkwardly; consider rewriting more concisely to improve clarity and focus on the specific comparison between metal salts and bacterial inhibition.
The term âbacteriasâ is incorrect; use the proper plural âbacteriaâ to maintain scientific accuracy in terminology.
The discussion of cell wall differences is descriptive; integrate critical analysis of how these structural features influence metal ion diffusion and antimicrobial activity.
The analysis of E. coli results includes clear interpretation of standard deviation, aiding understanding of data variability and consistency.
Background citations include generic web sources (e.g., Rotax Metals) without peer-reviewed authority; replace or supplement with scholarly articles to strengthen the literature foundation.
The diagram lacks a numbered figure caption and formal citation format; add a labeled caption with source details directly below the image for clarity.
Justification for selecting a 1 M concentration is brief; provide literature support or preliminary data to substantiate why this concentration was appropriate for antimicrobial testing.
The title page clearly states the investigation focus, listing both the metal salts and bacterial species, which provides an immediate and accurate context for the research.
Entries for âWork citedâ and âAppendicesâ lack page numbers in the table of contents; ensure all listed sections include accurate page references.
Graph 1 lacks axis labels (including units) and a legend explaining error bars; complete all graph elements for unambiguous data presentation.
The ANOVA description omits discussion of test assumptions (e.g., variance homogeneity); address these assumptions to validate the statistical analysis.
A word count is provided at the beginning of the report, correctly meeting IB formatting requirements and demonstrating awareness of the prescribed limit.
The contents page is comprehensive and logically organized, facilitating reader navigation through sections and subsections.
Including a photograph of preliminary results demonstrates reflective practice and supports the methodological refinement to agar-well diffusion.
Part 2 subheading clearly delineates the agar plate preparation and inoculation procedure, enhancing the reportâs structural clarity.
Table 5 contains extensive pairwise data that is hard to parse; highlight significant comparisons in text or use bold formatting to direct attention to key results.
Text states use of â1 Mâ solutions but Appendix C calculates 0.05 M; resolve this inconsistency in solution concentration to ensure methodological rigor.
The ethical rationale focuses on resource minimization but neglects discussion of biosafety protocols and ethical handling of microbial cultures; expand this section accordingly.
Table 1 systematically identifies safety, ethical, and environmental controls, demonstrating comprehensive planning of experimental variables.
Table 3 presents means and standard deviations clearly, demonstrating effective use of source calculators and statistical summary methods.
Cross-references to treatment tables are inconsistent; ensure all table numbers and references align correctly to avoid reader confusion.
Graph 2 effectively compares M. luteus inhibition zones, visually reinforcing numerical trends across metal salts.
The evaluation of comparative results remains descriptive; deepen the discussion by linking outcomes back to the original hypothesis and underlying biochemical mechanisms.
Identifying real-world applications, particularly medical device relevance, demonstrates strong engagement with the researchâs broader impact.
The transition to the conclusion is clearly signposted with an appropriate subheading, maintaining report coherence.
The conclusion reiterates mechanisms but does not explicitly revisit the research question or suggest future experimental directions; strengthen linkage to the studyâs aims.
Works cited entries show inconsistent formatting and missing retrieval dates; apply a uniform citation style throughout the reference list.
Duplicate listing for Mendes et al. appears in the references; remove redundancies to enhance clarity and accuracy of the works cited.
The reference on this page appears out of alphabetical sequence; reorder entries to maintain consistent reference listing structure.
